item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, I like this one. The buildup, the documents slowly revealing more, the ironic twist at the end; definate +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The ones with mortgages, the bank might be considered legal owners.
Otherwise, abandoned properties often fall to the city. Or county. Whoever you're paying property taxes to, essentially.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing beside remains, barren and bare…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did find a bit of a disconnect between his submitting written articles to LIFE magazine to becoming the greatest acting phenom (maybe submitted talent reels would make more sense?

I don't think he really gave a shit about either, he just wanted to be recognized. To me, he's "the greatest acting phenom" because all the people he sucked up in the ritual were actors/actresses.

One thing confuses me (mostly because I am me) is that as a nobody, how did he get access to that upper-echelon stage/awards show to do his thing?

In an earlier draft, he was sort of invited, he knew a handful of people there, but they sort of disregarded him since he hadn't actually made anything of note.

Or he just snuck in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did find a bit of a disconnect between his submitting written articles to LIFE magazine to becoming the greatest acting phenom (maybe submitted talent reels would make more sense? Or is it that he truly accepted that dumb stupid writing just wasn't his thing, but ACTING! was where he truly shone, of course!)

One thing confuses me (mostly because I am me) is that as a nobody, how did he get access to that upper-echelon stage/awards show to do his thing? Everyone seemed to know who he was as he came on stage. Maybe not knowing is the friends we make along the way, but I felt that was a big "wait, what, how?" Was that something facilitated by the recipe section of the ritual?

I also really want to know how he effed up the final ritual to cause his specific identity to become unknowable (so close!). It's a testament to the story that I was that engaged!

Awesome article, rounderhouse!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great article.

Although there's a very geeky side of me which wonders what happens, legally speaking, to properties of which no one can find an owner. Any lawyers in the house?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And not even properly remembered- no one will ever know his name or face.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice! I hope we'll learn more the character of
+ Blurred Out Name
- Since I started reading SCP articles I've begun copying and pasting everything obsessively and it's destroying my keyboard

Victor Snyder

in the future. A lot of great opportunities for spinoff tales in this piece!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: gj bud

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not bad, but it's not great, either. The execution is technically solid enough, and the base idea is fine. However, by the mid-point of the CCTV log, I knew almost exactly where the article was going, and nothing really challenged my expectations. The punch of him becoming ToMT guy was lessened by mentioning that no further details are given in the description.

The only thing that sort of threw me for a loop is that some of the documentation indicates that he should be some sort of mutli-disciplinary threat, but instead he's just "the greatest actor of the 21st century." That, and the fact that people still submit writing to LIFE.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the gist of it, yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, if I'm getting the upshot here…

+ Show
- Hide

…hundreds of movie stars were wiped from existence so that this guy could steal their fame, but the "ritual" somehow went wrong/he overdid it and he became, ironically, anomalously UNfamous?

I like this, particularly how the story unfolds through the documents. Good example of "show, don't tell." I would say that the interview felt a little anticlimactic, since it didn't really reveal anything we didn't already know. Still, an easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really really like this one! The subtle character intro by means of the apartment investigation was super effective, and I adore the style of the ritual instructions.

My only complaint is that the final interview felt like it needed an extra 'punch' of an ending, so to speak. Definitely not an objectionable ending, but it makes the overall read peter out towards the finale.

For everything else, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really dig this, but that isn't really a surprise considering that memory and "long, but not forgotten" are some of my favorite things to explore in terms of themes.

This was a fun puzzle to work out. The pieces fit together very nicely without being overly expository or hand-holdy, and the end is punchy and effective. This is certainly a work to be prooud of. An easy upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Solid article all around. I particularly liked the ritual instructions, and the implication that the guy sacrificed all those famous actors and actresses just to be remembered is pretty damn chilling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's heavy, no doubt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Look Upon My Works, Ye Mighty

And Despair

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, the price of fame.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohhhhh I get it, his name has the same effect as his face! That's pretty cool!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad this is published! So many things about this: the title, the implicit characterization of the man, as well as his ironic fate were all well-crafted and made for a compelling narrative.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It’s okay take your time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OZYMANDIAS

Starring: … what's his name again?

Directed by Rounderhouse

Produced by Nicolini, Cap. Kirby, Aismallard, Gecko, Tufto, ellie3, ch00, vezaz, and probably a bunch of other people who looked at this in its very different forms.
A special thanks to Yossi — this was originally meant as a collab with him, but it didn't quite pan out.

Yurt Productions


Image: Public Domain
